Title: The Atlantis World (The Origin Mystery #3)
Author: A.G. Riddle
My Review:
The book is an easy read.

The plot is amateurish. There's zero tension whenever the characters is in mortal danger since they've survived so many near death experiences and deaths that I could not be bothered to care whenever either of the characters was in danger. The series started with an interesting premise but the execution and the resolution is mediocre.


*** SPOILERS AHEAD ****


Both of the protagonists are about as cliched as you can imagine, the jack of all trades secret agent and the damsel in distress scientist. I finished the trilogy because the first book is actually decent and has an interesting premise but by the third book the story gets tiresome. The characters don't evolve (which is such a shame since the book is about evolution) and previously unmentioned past experience magically surface up to resolve critical plot points. None of the characters have to step outside of their comfort zone and the magical super advanced alien race that shows up doesn't actually stay to help humanity recover after the protagonists save them. WTF? If I was going to save the universe and half my planet was wrecked because of that I'd at least ask the super advance aliens to lend a hand in rebuilding the planet but nope they just leave.

Title: Artemis
Author: Andy Weir
My Review:
The story overall is okay. It's different than the usual world ending stakes of scifi books. It's a small self contained story, sort of in the same universe as the Martian (you'll know when you run across a familiar character).

Props for writing the book that has a woman of middle eastern descent as the protagonist but you can tell that Andy Weir struggled to create a believable character. At times it feels like Jazz is a teenage boy, I chalked it up to the character being young but mid way through the book you find out that she's 26. So yeah, the fact that the protagonist comes across as an immature teenage boy diminishes the quality of the book significantly.

You could argue that well, may be that's how life on the moon is like but none of the other characters behave like Jazz, she is the least mature character in the book and add to the fact that she's written as a savant who can master whatever she wants in an evening she doesn't come across as believable. I guess Weir was going for the good will hunting in space kind of vibe but with a woman, but it doesn't land as well.

Title: The Bone Season (The Bone Season, #1)
Author: Samantha Shannon
My Review:
This might be the worst book I've ever read, and I've read half of the first Twilight book.

The review is long and I ramble on due to my frustration.
TL;DR : Harry potter had a threesome with Katniss Everdeen and that emo chick from Twilight. The result is just as horrific as you would expect.

I really really wanted to like this book. It starts of on the right foot, sort of. It's slow and a huge chunk of the book is focused on building this alternate reality. The world building part of the book is done well enough and almost gives you hope that there will be an act 2 worthy of all the time you just spent reading act 1.

That does not happen. What happens next is just plain stupid. There's no other way to express it. The way the first half of the book is structured, a lot of world building with very little in terms of character development, the expectation is that there will some sort of character development in act 2. That the author will make me like at least one of the 200 characters that have been introduced half way through the book. Nope.

I am just supposed to assume that Miss Paige, our heroine is a likable character and I must root for her. When all I want to do is stop her and ask if she's mentally insane for putting so many people that she apparently wants to protect in danger. I am also supposed to accept that the people that she continues to put in danger because she intentionally wants to be stupid all look up her to defend them. That they will convince her to participate in a plan and in the next five seconds ask her what they must do next. Seriously dude? five seconds ago this woman didn't know about your plan now she's the leader? WTF.

The dialogue is cringe worthy. Act 2 is all about different people convincing our heroine to be a part of their plan, while simultaneously handing her the reins to that very same plan. This is also the time when she falls in love with the man she is supposed to hate. Obviously he's not a vampire. He only drinks blood as a medicine. So he's totally not a vampire. Got that? So it's totally okay to fall in love with this guy. Also he's at least a thousand years old and probably dead.

The plot can be summarized as Harry Potter having a threesome with Katniss Everdeen and Bella Swan. The result is something that has the worst parts of the hunger games, all the parts of Twilight and none of the good parts of Harry Potter.

I can't believe that someone got a lot of money to write this, then someone got a lot of money to edit this, then someone made a truck load of money selling this to a filmmaker. Who will now spend hundreds of millions of dollars on making a movie out this that will make billions. It's all very very sad. None of that is as tragic as Samantha Shannon being compared to J.K. Rowling who even at her worst is magnitudes better than Miss Shannon at her best.
